content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

May 20, 1997


James R. Sutton
Law Offices of Nielsen, Merksamer
Parrinello, Mueller & Naylor
591 Redwood Highway, #4000
Mill Valley, California 94941

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-97-226

Dear Mr. Sutton:

This letter is a response to your request, on behalf of the California Republican Party, about the status of the Raper Advice Letter, No. I-97-036. That letter, among other things, addressed the solicitation of contributions to a political party committee by a candidate or elected official on a lobbyist who finances, engages, or is authorized to engage in lobbying the candidate or elected official's agency. In the Raper letter, we advised that Section 85704 does not "explicitly prohibit a candidate or elected official from soliciting or arranging contributions for a political party from any lobbyist." (Ibid., emphasis in original.)

The Commission discussed the Raper letter at its April 1997 meeting, and determined that the conclusion in the letter would stand. Please note that whether we would reach a similar conclusion in any given situation depends upon the facts of each particular situation, as posed in a formal advice request. Also, we wish to reemphasize most strongly the cautionary note in the same letter about the prohibition against earmarking contributions. (See Section 85703.)

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: John Vergelli
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JCV:ak

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660
